Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanction, Or In The Alternative Evidentiary Sanction
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael S. Dorsi, esq. (415-795-3579) for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued. Discussed; Second amended complaint is dismissed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs BRADLEY & COMPANY, LLC and MICHAEL BRADLEY's Motion For Terminating Sanction, Or In The Alternative Evidentiary Sanction:

Defendants Michael D. Bradley and Bradley & Company, LLC's motion for terminating sanctions is granted, the Second Amended Complaint is ordered stricken, and Plaintiff is ordered to pay Defendants $4,085.43 for the reasonable expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred by Defendants as a result of her conduct. (Code Civ. Proc. 2023.030(d), 2025.450(h).)

Plaintiff Kristen Eckert failed to appear at her properly noticed deposition or to serve responses to Defendants' interrogatories. Despite attempts by Defendants' counsel to obtain a new deposition date, Plaintiff's attorney never offered an alternative date, and did not respond to attempts to confer regarding her interrogatory responses. The Court granted Defendants' unopposed motion to compel Plaintiff to appear for her deposition, to respond to interrogatories, and to pay sanctions. Despite the Court's order, Plaintiff did not serve responses to Defendants' interrogatories, and she failed to obey the Court's order that she appear for her deposition on the date ordered by the Court. She also failed to respond to requests for admissions served by Defendants, and the Court granted Defendants' unopposed motion to deem those requests admitted. Finally, she did not file any opposition to the instant motion. As Defendants aptly observe, Plaintiff has abandoned her own case.

Plaintiff's repeated failures to provide timely discovery responses and to comply with the Court's orders constitute willful, repeated, and egregious misuse of the discovery process that warrants the imposition of terminating sanctions. (See, e.g., CREED-21 v. City of Wildomar (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 690, 701-703; Department of Forestry and Fire Protection v. Howell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 154, 197-198; Los Defensores, Inc. v. Gomez (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 377, 390-392.) No less severe sanction would be effective to produce full compliance with the discovery rules or to ensure Defendants receive a fair trial.

Order and Judgment are signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
